problem.title: Factor Combinations
problem.description: Numbers can be regarded as the product of their factors. Given an integer n, return all possible combinations of its factors.

example.explanation: 12 = 2×6 = 2×2×3 = 3×4. The number itself (12) is not included.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Backtracking
solutions.description: Use backtracking to enumerate all factor combinations by trying every divisor from a given start value up to n. At each recursive call, if n divides evenly by i, we include i in the current combination and recurse with n/i. The key insight is passing start=i to each recursive call to avoid duplicate combinations (e.g., 2×6 and 6×2 are the same).
